description: Fetch wild-type T4 lysozyme (PDB: 2LZM) and the engineered L99A cavity mutant bound to benzene (PDB: 4W52). Superimpose 4W52 onto 2LZM. Measure the distance between the Cα atom of residue 99 in 2LZM and the Cα atom of residue 99 in 4W52 to verify backbone rigidity. Identify the benzene ligand (residue name BNZ) in 4W52 and find all protein residues in 4W52 with at least one atom within 4.0 Å of the benzene ligand. Render a high-quality visualization (PNG) showing the superimposed structures, highlighting the side chains of residue 99 in both structures and the bound benzene ligand. Save the figure to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/t4_cavity_superposition.png. Write a structural analysis report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/t4_cavity_report.txt containing the measured Cα-Cα distance for residue 99 (in Ångströms) and a list of the cavity-lining residue numbers in 4W52 (those within 4.0 Å of the benzene).
